title: Wallen festival
content: During Wallen festival, the Waag opens its doors for an exhibition of artists who deconstruct and reimagine technology. Come and visit!
snippet: During Wallen festival, the Waag opens its doors for an exhibition of artists who deconstruct and reimagine technology. Come and visit!

title: Dien je burgerinitiatief in voor de Citizen Science call van de NWA
content: We nodigen samenwerkingen tussen burgercollectieven en kennisinstellingen die gezamenlijk een onderzoek van maximaal 1 jaar willen uitvoeren van harte uit om een voorstel in te dienen.
snippet: We nodigen samenwerkingen tussen burgercollectieven en kennisinstellingen die gezamenlijk een onderzoek van maximaal 1 jaar willen uitvoeren van harte uit om een voorstel in te dienen.

title: Protect the North Sea: Transparency, citizen science and action for the Dogger Bank
content: or some time now, economic and ecological interests have been at odds, with the result that ecology is always losing out.

title: How to be close to the sea?
content: Nature needs more voices and representatives to protect and monitor it. Join us at this workshop to help protect our water.
snippet: Nature needs more voices and representatives to protect and monitor it. Join us at this workshop to help protect our water.

title: Waterwise
content: Is the water clean enough to swim in? Why does water sometimes turn green? Join us on 6 August to measure and observe water quality, and discover for yourself what water can tell you.
snippet: Is the water clean enough to swim in? Why does water sometimes turn green? Join us on 6 August to measure and observe water quality, and discover for yourself what water can tell you.

title: Re-watch the State of the Internet 2026 with Fieke Jansen
content: On 10 April 2026, Waag Futurelab organised its annual lecture The State of the Internet, featuring Fieke Jansen, co-founder of the critical infrastructure lab of the University of Amsterdam.
snippet: On 10 April 2026, Waag Futurelab organised its annual lecture The State of the Internet, featuring Fieke Jansen, co-founder of the critical infrastructure lab of the University of Amsterdam.

title: A beginner’s guide to radio mesh networks
content: Are you interested in mesh networks, but don’t know where to start? This guide will help you start discovering the world of radio mesh networks.
snippet: Are you interested in mesh networks, but don’t know where to start? This guide will help you start discovering the world of radio mesh networks.

title: Exhibition: Short Circuit
content: The artists in the exhibition Short Circuit break technology down and build new ideas from the digital debris. See you there?
snippet: The artists in the exhibition Short Circuit break technology down and build new ideas from the digital debris. See you there?

title: The technological city
content: Join The technological city walk on 2 July and discover how technology is woven into everyday life in the city. Walk through different places in the neighbourhood and take a closer look at the systems, devices and data around you. What role does technology play in the city — and who is it really for?

title: Sign up now for the Teacher Camps at Waag Futurelab
content: Sign up now for the Teacher Maker Camp and Teacher Tech Camp at Waag Futurelab for the 2026–2027 academic year. During these two-day training sessions, you’ll discover new ways to creatively incorporate technology and maker education into your classroom.

title: Remembering Steve Kurtz
content: Remembering Steve Kurtz is an evening devoted to exploring and celebrating the life and work of Steve Kurtz, pioneer in bio art and tactical media.
snippet: is an evening devoted to exploring and celebrating the life and work of Steve Kurtz, pioneer in bio art and tactical media.

title: Why we moved our Bluesky data to Eurosky
content: Our presence in the ATmosphere – Bluesky’s protocol – is no longer dependent on the platform’s standard infrastructure. Instead, our data now runs on Eurosky’s Personal Data Server (PDS).
snippet: Our presence in the ATmosphere – Bluesky’s protocol – is no longer dependent on the platform’s standard infrastructure. Instead, our data now runs on Eurosky’s Personal Data Server (PDS).

title: Hyperlink 2026
content: Join us on Saturday 6 June for Hyperlink by PublicSpaces and Waag Futurelab! In workshops, talks and an exhibition, you will discover how we can make technology work for our democracy again.
snippet: Join us on Saturday 6 June for Hyperlink by PublicSpaces and Waag Futurelab! In workshops, talks and an exhibition, you will discover how we can make technology work for our democracy again.

title: Democracy in the Making: Creative Methods for Youth and Educators
content: Democracy in the Making: Creative Methods for Youth and Educators is a handbook for teachers to facilitate discussions about everyday democracy in the classroom and help young using creative methods.
